title: Package Development
description: To include Livewire components in a Laravel package, you'll need to register them in your package's service provider.

sections.title: #Single-file and multi-file components
sections.content:
For single-file (SFC) and multi-file (MFC) components, use addNamespace in your service provider's boot() method:
This registers all SFC and MFC components in your package's resources/views/livewire directory under the mypackage namespace.
Usage:

examples.code: use Livewire\Livewire; public function boot(): void{ Livewire::addNamespace( namespace: 'mypackage', viewPath: __DIR__ . '/../resources/views/livewire', );}

sections.title: #Class-based components
sections.content:
For class-based components, you'll need to provide additional parameters and register your views with Laravel:
Your component's render() method should reference the view using Laravel's package namespace syntax:
Usage:

examples.code: use Livewire\Livewire; public function boot(): void{ Livewire::addNamespace( namespace: 'mypackage', classNamespace: 'MyVendor\\MyPackage\\Livewire', classPath: __DIR__ . '/Livewire', classViewPath: __DIR__ . '/../resources/views/livewire', );  $this->loadViewsFrom(__DIR__ . '/../resources/views', 'my-package');}

sections.title: #File naming
sections.content: The ⚡ emoji prefix used in Livewire component filenames can cause issues with Composer when publishing packages. For package development, avoid using the bolt emoji in your component filenames—use counter.blade.php instead of ⚡counter.blade.php.
